sections.content:
I see the middle row changed. Before:
Row 26: RRR c ww c bb c bb c ww c SS c pp c gg c SS c gg c gg c NNN
After:
Row 26: RRR c bb c bb c ww c SS c pp c gg c SS c gg c gg c ww c NNN
The 'ww' at the beginning moved to the end. So clicking the red cross shifts the middle row LEFT (counterclockwise), similar to level 1.
The sequence was: w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g
After shift left by 1: b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g, w
Now I need to understand the full structure. There seem to be:
1. Top row (5 squares): S, b, p, Y, p - controlled by top crosses
2. Middle row (10 squares): w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g - controlled by middle crosses
3. Bottom row (10 squares): w, S, b, w, b, S, p, b, g, S - controlled by bottom crosses
4. Left column (between rows): p, g, w, w, Y, g, S
5. Right column (between rows): b, S, w, g, w, b
6. Bottom row (4 squares): S, S, p, p, g
Wait, looking more carefully at the structure, I think there are actually more rows/columns. Let me re-examine.
Looking at the ascii:
- Row 17-18: top horizontal row (between top crosses)
- Row 20-21: p (left), b (right)
- Row 23-24: g (left), S (right)
- Row 26-27: middle horizontal row (between middle crosses)
- Row 29-30: w (left), w (right)
- Row 32-33: w (left), g (right)
- Row 35-36: bottom horizontal row (between bottom crosses)
- Row 38-39: Y (left), w (right)
- Row 41-42: g (left), b (right)
- Row 44-45: S, S, p, p, g (5 squares)
So the structure is:
- 3 horizontal rows that can be shifted
- A left column of 7 squares (p, g, w, w, Y, g, S) - but wait, the Y at (38,23) has a yellow frame around it
- A right column of 6 squares (b, S, w, g, w, b)
- A bottom row of 5 squares (S, S, p, p, g)
Actually, I think the left and right columns might also be shiftable. Let me check if there are crosses controlling them.
Looking at the crosses:
- Top: R at (16,19), N at (16,38) - controls top row
- Middle: R at (25,13), N at (25,47) - controls middle row
- Bottom: R at (34,13), N at (34,47) - controls bottom row
There are no crosses for the left/right columns or the bottom row. Maybe those are fixed.
The yellow frames are at:
- (17,32): around Y in the top row - this is the 4th square in the top row
- (38,23): around Y in the left column - this is the 5th square in the left column
Wait, but the Y at (17,32) is actually a 2x2 yellow square, not a frame. Let me re-examine.
Looking at the segmentation:
- ID 16: color=Y, pixels=4, pos=(17,32)-(18,33) - this is a 2x2 yellow square
- ID 23, 25, 39, 41, 47, 49: color=Y, pixels=1 each - these are 1x1 yellow markers
- ID 62 (or similar): color=Y, pixels=4, pos=(38,23)-(39,24) - this is another 2x2 yellow square
So there are two 2x2 yellow squares and 6 1x1 yellow markers. The 1x1 markers form a frame around the center.
Actually, looking at the ascii again:
Row 25: cccRRccccccccccccccccccYccYcccccccccNNcc
Row 28: cccRRccccccccccccccccccYccYcccccccccNNcc
Row 34: cccRRccccccccccccccccccYccYcccccccccNNcc
Row 37: cccRRccccccccccccccccccYccYcccccccccNNcc
The Y markers are at columns 34 and 37, rows 25, 28, 34, 37. They form a rectangular frame.
Hmm, but the yellow squares at (17,32) and (38,23) are actual colored squares that are part of the puzzle, not frames. Let me reconsider.
Looking at the top row:
Row 17: RRR c SS c bb c pp c YY c pp c NNN
The YY at (17,32) is a 2x2 yellow square, which is one of the colored squares in the top row.
Looking at the left column:
Row 38: cccccccccccccYYccccccccccwwcccccccccccccc
The YY at (38,23) is a 2x2 yellow square in the left column.
So the yellow squares are just regular colored squares. The 1x1 Y markers form a frame around the center area.
Now, what's the goal? In level 1, the goal was to shift the perimeter until the pattern matched some target. In level 2, maybe the goal is to shift the three rows until the columns match some pattern.
Let me think about this differently. The "38 target actions" hint suggests the human solution takes about 38 clicks. With 3 rows of 10 squares each, there are 30 squares to potentially shift. If each row needs to be shifted to a specific position, that could take up to 9 shifts per row (for a 10-square row), totaling up to 27 shifts. But 38 is more than that.
Maybe the left/right columns and bottom row are also shiftable, just with different controls. Or maybe the goal involves clicking individual squares.
Let me try clicking the green cross on the middle row to see if it shifts in the opposite direction.
